author: śrī⋅śaṅkarācārya
verses:
devi! sureśvari! bhagavati! gaṅge!
tri⋅bhuvana⋅tāriṇi! tarala⋅taraṅge
śaṅkara⋅mauli⋅nivāsini! vimale!
mama matirāstāṁ tava pada⋅kamale 

bhāgīrathi! sukha⋅dāyini! mātas⋅
tava jala⋅mahimā nigame⋅khyātaḥ
nāhaṁ jāne tava mahimānaṁ
trāhi kṛpāmayi! māmajñānam 

hari⋅pāda⋅padma⋅vihāriṇi! gaṅge!
hima⋅vidhu⋅muktā⋅dhavala⋅taraṅge !
dūrīkuru mama duṣkṛti⋅bhāraṁ
kuru kṛpayā bhava⋅sāgara⋅pāram 

tava jalamamalaṁ jena nipītaṁ
parama⋅padaṁ khalu tena gṛhītam
mātargaṅge! tvayi jo bhaktaḥ
kila taṁ draṣṭuṁ na jamaḥ śaktaḥ 

patitoddhāriṇi! jāhnavi! gaṅge!
khaṇḍita⋅girivara⋅maṇḍita⋅bhaṅge !
bhīṣma⋅janani! khalu muni⋅vara⋅kanye!
patitoddhāriṇi! tri⋅bhuvana⋅dhanye! 

kalpalatāmiva phaladāṁ loke
praṇamati jastvāṁ na patati śoke
pārāvāra⋅vihāriṇi! gaṅge!
vibudha⋅vādhu⋅kṛta⋅taralāpāṅge! 

tava kṛpayā cet srotaḥsnātaḥ
punarapi jaṭhare so‛pi na jātaḥ
naraka⋅nivāriṇi! jāhnavi! gaṅge!
kaluṣa⋅vināśini! mahimottuṅge! 

parilasadaṅge! puṇya⋅taraṅge!
jaya jaya jāhnavi! karuṇāpāṅge !
indra⋅mukuṭamaṇi⋅rājita⋅caraṇe!
sukhade! śubhade! sevaka⋅śaraṇe! 

rogaṁ śokaṁ tāpaṁ pāpaṁ
hara me bhagavati! kumati⋅kalāpam
tribhuvana⋅sāre! vasudhā⋅hāre!
tvamasi gatirmama khalu saṁsāre 

alakānande! paramānande!
kuru mayi karuṇāṁ kātara⋅vandye !
tava taṭa⋅nikaṭe jasya nivāsaḥ
khalu vaikuṇṭhe tasya nivāsaḥ

varamiha nīre kamaṭho mīnaḥ
kimvā tīre śaraṭaḥ kṣīṇaḥ
athavā gavyūti⋅śvapaco dīna⋅
stava na hi dūre nṛpatiḥ kulīnaḥ

bho bhuvaneśvari! puṇye! dhanye!
devi! dravamayi! munivara⋅kanye !
gaṅgā⋅stavamimamamalaṁ nityaṁ
paṭhati naro jaḥ sa jayati satyam

jeṣāṁ hṛdaye gaṅgā⋅bhakti⋅
steṣāṁ bhavati sadā sukha⋅muktiḥ
madhura⋅manohara⋅pajjhaṭikābhiḥ
paramānanda⋅kalita⋅lalitābhiḥ

gaṅgāstotramidaṁ bhavasāraṁ
vāñchita⋅phaladaṁ vigalita⋅bhāram
śaṅkara⋅sevaka⋅śaṅkara⋅racitaṁ
paṭhati ca viṣayīdamiti samāptam
en_translation:
O Devi! O Mistress of the gods! O Goddess! O Gaṅgā! O Deliverer of the three worlds! O You with restless waves! O You who dwell on the head of Śaṅkara! O Pure One! May my mind always rest at Your lotus feet.

O Bhāgīrathī! O Giver of happiness! O Mother! The glory of Your water is famous in the Vedas. I do not know Your glory; O Merciful One! Protect me, who am ignorant.

O Gaṅgā, who sports at the lotus feet of Hari! Your waves are white like snow, the moon, and pearls. Please remove the burden of my sins; by Your mercy, help me cross the ocean of material existence.

He by whom Your pure water is drunk has indeed attained the supreme abode. O Mother Gaṅgā! He who is a devotee of Yours—Yama is unable to even look at him.

O Deliverer of the fallen! O Jāhnavī! O Gaṅgā! Your meandering flow is adorned by the rocks of the King of Mountains (Himālaya). O Mother of Bhīṣma! O Daughter of the great sage (Jahnu)! O Deliverer of the fallen! O You who are glorious in the three worlds!

You are like a wish-fulfilling creeper granting fruits in this world. He who bows to You does not suffer grief. O Gaṅgā, who sports in the ocean! You are gazed upon by the restless eyes of the celestial damsels.

If, by Your mercy, one bathes in Your current, he is not born again in a womb. O Preventer of hell! O Jāhnavī! O Gaṅgā! O Destroyer of sins! O You of towering glory!

O You of shining limbs! O Wave of piety! Glory, glory to You, O Jāhnavī! O You of merciful glance! Your feet are illuminated by the jewels of Indra's crown. O Giver of happiness! O Giver of auspiciousness! O Shelter of Your servants!

O Bhagavatī! Please take away my disease, sorrow, suffering, sin, and heaps of wicked thoughts. O Essence of the three worlds! O Necklace of the Earth! You are indeed my refuge in this material world.

O Alakānandā! O Supreme Bliss! Be merciful to me, O You who are worshipped by the distressed! He who resides near Your banks—his residence is verily in Vaikuṇṭha.

It is better to be a tortoise or a fish in Your waters here, or a tiny lizard on Your bank, or a wretched dog-eater living near You, than to be a king of noble birth far away from You.

O Queen of the Universe! O Pious One! O Blessed One! O Goddess! O Embodiment of water! O Daughter of the great sage! The person who recites this spotless prayer to Gaṅgā daily—he truly becomes victorious.

Those in whose heart devotion to Gaṅgā dwells—for them there is always happiness and liberation. This prayer is adorned with sweet, captivating rhyming verses, filled with supreme bliss.

This Gaṅgā Stotram is the essence of worldly existence, the bestower of desired fruits, and the remover of burdens. Composed by Śaṅkara (achārya), the servant of Śaṅkara (Lord Śiva). Whoever reads it, his materi al entanglement ends.
